title: cynometra craibii
intro: Cynometra craibii, also known as the Craib's ironwood, is a species of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antimalarial and antimicrobial activities, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cynometra craibii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional medicine, the tree's bark and seeds are often used to prepare herbal remedies, including decoctions and infusions, which are administered orally to harness its medicinal properties.
